task.name: metasploitable3 dsvw phpldapadmin-remote-dump metasploit-emulator shellshock-lab heartbleed-lab xvwa railsgoat-alt bwapp-alt metasploitable3-alt mongodb-noauth gitlab10 es56 sonar67

filters.term: host=172.30.40.5 and apply_overrides=0 and levels=chmlgf and rows=1000 and min_qod=0 and sort-reverse=severity first=1

task.name: metasploitable3 dsvw phpldapadmin-remote-dump metasploit-emulator shellshock-lab heartbleed-lab xvwa railsgoat-alt bwapp-alt metasploitable3-alt mongodb-noauth gitlab10 es56 sonar67

target.name: metasploitable3 dsvw phpldapadmin-remote-dump metasploit-emulator shellshock-lab heartbleed-lab xvwa railsgoat-alt bwapp-alt metasploitable3-alt mongodb-noauth gitlab10 es56 sonar67

result.name: Operating System (OS) End of Life (EOL) Detection

nvt.name: Operating System (OS) End of Life (EOL) Detection
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=The Operating System (OS) on the remote host has reached the end of life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.|insight=|affected=|impact=An EOL version of an OS is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unfixed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.|solution=Update the OS on the remote host to a version which is still supported and receiving security updates by the vendor.
Note / Important: Please create an override for this result if the target host is a:
- Windows system with Extended Security Updates (ESU)
- System with additional 3rd-party / non-vendor security updates like e.g. from 'TuxCare', 'Freexian Extended LTS' or similar|vuldetect=Checks if an EOL version of an OS is present on the target host.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
Update the OS on the remote host to a version which is still supported and receiving security updates by the vendor.
Note / Important: Please create an override for this result if the target host is a:
- Windows system with Extended Security Updates (ESU)
- System with additional 3rd-party / non-vendor security updates like e.g. from 'TuxCare', 'Freexian Extended LTS' or similar

result.description:
The "Debian GNU/Linux" Operating System on the remote host has reached the end of life.
CPE: cpe:/o:debian:debian_linux:8 Installed version, build or SP: 8 EOL date: 2020-06-30 EOL info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Debian_releases#Release_table

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.49 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.49 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2021-34798: NULL pointer dereference in httpd core
- CVE-2021-39275: ap_escape_quotes buffer overflow
- CVE-2021-40438: mod_proxy SSRF|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.48 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.49 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.49 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.54 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.54 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2022-26377: mod_proxy_ajp: Possible request smuggling
- CVE-2022-28614: Read beyond bounds via ap_rwrite()
- CVE-2022-28615: Read beyond bounds in ap_strcmp_match()
- CVE-2022-29404: Denial of service in mod_lua r:parsebody
- CVE-2022-30556: Information disclosure in mod_lua with websockets
- CVE-2022-31813: mod_proxy X-Forwarded-For dropped by hop-by-hop mechanism|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.53 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.54 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.54 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 - 2.4.55 HTTP Request Smuggling Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 - 2.4.55 HTTP Request Smuggling Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a HTTP request smuggling vulnerability.|insight=Some mod_proxy configurations allow a HTTP Request Smuggling attack.
Configurations are affected when mod_proxy is enabled along with some form of RewriteRule or ProxyPassMatch in which a non-specific pattern matches some portion of the user-supplied request-target (URL) data and is then re-inserted into the proxied request-target using variable substitution.|affected=Apache HTTP Server versions 2.4.0 through 2.4.55.|impact=Request splitting/smuggling could result in bypass of access controls in the proxy server, proxying unintended URLs to existing origin servers, and cache poisoning.|solution=Update to version 2.4.56 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.56 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 - 2.4.46 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 - 2.4.46 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2020-35452: mod_auth_digest possible stack overflow by one null byte
- CVE-2021-26690: mod_session NULL pointer dereference
- CVE-2021-26691: mod_session response handling heap overflow|affected=Apache HTTP Server versions 2.4.0 to 2.4.46 on Linux.|impact=- CVE-2020-35452: A specially crafted Digest nonce can cause a stack overflow in mod_auth_digest.
- CVE-2021-26690: A specially crafted Cookie header handled by mod_session can cause a NULL pointer dereference and crash, leading to a possible Denial Of Service.
- CVE-2021-26691: A specially crafted SessionHeader sent by an origin server could cause a heap overflow.|solution=Update to version 2.4.48 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.48 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server Multiple Vulnerabilities (Jun 2017) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server Multiple Vulnerabilities (Jun 2017) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=Multiple flaws exist as,
- The mod_mime can read one byte past the end of a buffer when sending a malicious Content-Type response header.
- The mod_ssl may dereference a NULL pointer when third-party modules call ap_hook_process_connection() during an HTTP request to an HTTPS port.
- An use of the ap_get_basic_auth_pw() by third-party modules outside of the authentication phase may lead to authentication requirements being bypassed.|affected=Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x before 2.2.33 and 2.4.x before 2.4.26.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to bypass authentication and perform unauthorized actions, cause a denial-of-service condition and gain access to potentially sensitive information.|solution=Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.2.33 or 2.4.26 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.2.33 or 2.4.26 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.26 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.60 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.60 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2024-36387: Denial of Service (DoS) by Null pointer in websocket over HTTP/2
- CVE-2024-38473: Proxy encoding problem
- CVE-2024-38474: Weakness with encoded question marks in backreferences
- CVE-2024-38475: Weakness in mod_rewrite when first segment of substitution matches filesystem path
- CVE-2024-38476: May use exploitable/malicious backend application output to run local handlers via internal redirect
- CVE-2024-38477: Crash resulting in DoS in mod_proxy via a malicious request
- CVE-2024-39573: mod_rewrite proxy handler substitution|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.59 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.60 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.60 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server Multiple Vulnerabilities (Apr 2018) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server Multiple Vulnerabilities (Apr 2018) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=Multiple flaws exist due to:
- Apache HTTP Server fails to correctly generate the nonce sent to prevent reply attacks.
- Misconfigured mod_session variable, HTTP_SESSION.
- Apache HTTP Server fails to sanitize the expression specified in '<FilesMatch>'.
- An error in Apache HTTP Server 'mod_authnz_ldap' when configured with AuthLDAPCharsetConfig.
- Apache HTTP Server fails to sanitize against a specially crafted request.|affected=Apache HTTP Server versions from 2.4.1 to 2.4.4, 2.4.6, 2.4.7, 2.4.9, 2.4.10, 2.4.12, 2.4.16 to 2.4.18, 2.4.20, 2.4.23, 2.4.25 to 2.4.29.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow an attacker to replay HTTP requests across servers without detection, influence the user content, upload a malicious file, crash the Apache HTTP Server and perform denial of service attack.|solution=Update to version 2.4.30 or later. Please see the references for more information.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.30 or later. Please see the references for more information.

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.30 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server <= 2.4.51 Buffer Overflow Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server <= 2.4.51 Buffer Overflow Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a buffer overflow vulnerability.|insight=A carefully crafted request body can cause a buffer overflow in the mod_lua multipart parser (r:parsebody() called from Lua scripts).|affected=Apache HTTP Server versions through 2.4.51.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.52 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.52 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server <= 2.4.52 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server <= 2.4.52 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2022-22719: mod_lua Use of uninitialized value of in r:parsebody
- CVE-2022-22720: HTTP request smuggling vulnerability
- CVE-2022-22721: Possible buffer overflow with very large or unlimited LimitXMLRequestBody
- CVE-2022-23943: mod_sed: Read/write beyond bounds|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.52 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.53 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.53 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_auth_digest' Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_auth_digest' Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The flaw exists due to error in Apache 'mod_auth_digest' which does not properly initialize memory used to process 'Digest' type HTTP Authorization headers.|affected=Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x before 2.2.34 and 2.4.x before 2.4.27.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to cause the target service to crash. A remote user can obtain potentially sensitive information as well on the target system.|solution=Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.2.34 or 2.4.27 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.2.34 or 2.4.27 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.27 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.55 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.55 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2006-20001: mod_dav out of bounds read, or write of zero byte
- CVE-2022-36760: Possible request smuggling in mod_proxy_ajp
- CVE-2022-37436: mod_proxy allows a backend to trigger HTTP response splitting|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.54 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.55 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.55 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.7 - 2.4.51 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.7 - 2.4.51 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=A crafted URI sent to httpd configured as a forward proxy (ProxyRequests on) can cause a crash (NULL pointer dereference) or, for configurations mixing forward and reverse proxy declarations, can allow for requests to be directed to a declared Unix Domain Socket endpoint (Server Side Request Forgery).|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.7 through 2.4.51.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.52 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.52 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server Man-in-the-Middle Attack Vulnerability (Jul 2016) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server Man-in-the-Middle Attack Vulnerability (Jul 2016) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a man-in-the-middle attack vulnerability.|insight=The flaw is due to 'CGI Servlet' does not protect applications from the presence of untrusted client data in the 'HTTP_PROXY' environment variable.|affected=Apache HTTP Server through 2.4.23.
NOTE: Apache HTTP Server 2.2.32 is not vulnerable.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to redirect an application's outbound HTTP traffic to an arbitrary proxy server via a crafted proxy header in an HTTP request.|solution=Update to version 2.4.24, or 2.2.32, or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.24, or 2.2.32, or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.24 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server Denial of Service Vulnerability-02 (Apr 2018) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server Denial of Service Vulnerability-02 (Apr 2018) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service vulnerability.|insight=The flaw exists as the Apache HTTP Server fails to sanitize against a specially crafted HTTP request header.|affected=Apache HTTP Server versions 2.4.6, 2.4.7, 2.4.9, 2.4.10, 2.4.12, 2.4.16 through 2.4.18, 2.4.20, 2.4.23, and 2.4.25 through 2.4.29.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow an attacker to crash the Apache HTTP Server resulting in denial of service condition.|solution=Update to version 2.4.30 or later. Please see the references for more information.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.30 or later. Please see the references for more information.

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.30 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.59 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.59 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2023-38709: HTTP response splitting
- CVE-2024-24795: HTTP response splitting in multiple modules
- CVE-2024-27316: HTTP/2 DoS by memory exhaustion on endless continuation frames|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.58 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.59 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.59 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_auth_digest Access Control Bypass Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_auth_digest Access Control Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=In Apache HTTP Server, a race condition in mod_auth_digest when running in a threaded server could allow a user with valid credentials to authenticate using another username, bypassing configured access control restrictions.|insight=|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.39 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.39 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_auth_digest' DoS Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_auth_digest' DoS Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=The flaw exists due to insufficient handling of malicious input to 'mod_auth_digest'.|affected=Apache HTTP Server versions 2.4.23, 2.4.20, 2.4.18, 2.4.17, 2.4.16, 2.4.12, 2.4.10, 2.4.9, 2.4.7, 2.4.6, 2.4.4, 2.4.3, 2.4.2 and 2.4.1.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to cause a denial-of-service condition.|solution=Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.4.25 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.4.25 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.25 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server OPTIONS Memory Leak Vulnerability (Optionsbleed) - Version Check

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server OPTIONS Memory Leak Vulnerability (Optionsbleed) - Version Check
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server allows remote attackers to read secret data from process memory if the Limit directive can be set in a user's .htaccess file, or if httpd.conf has certain misconfigurations, aka Optionsbleed.|insight=Optionsbleed is a use after free error in the Apache HTTP Server that causes a corrupted Allow header to be constructed in response to HTTP OPTIONS requests. This can leak pieces of arbitrary memory from the server process that may contain secrets. The memory pieces change after multiple requests, so for a vulnerable host an arbitrary number of memory chunks can be leaked.
The bug appears if a webmaster tries to use the 'Limit' directive with an invalid HTTP method.
Example .htaccess:
<Limit abcxyz> </Limit>|affected=Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x versions up to 2.2.34 and 2.4.x below 2.4.28.|impact=The successful exploitation allows the attacker to read chunks of the host's memory.|solution=Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.4.28. For Apache HTTP Server running version 2.2.34 apply the patch linked in the references.
As a workaround the usage of .htaccess should be disabled completely via the 'AllowOverride None' directive within the webservers configuration. Furthermore all <Limit> statements within the webserver configuration needs to be verified for invalid HTTP methods.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution:
Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.4.28. For Apache HTTP Server running version 2.2.34 apply the patch linked in the references.
As a workaround the usage of .htaccess should be disabled completely via the 'AllowOverride None' directive within the webservers configuration. Furthermore all <Limit> statements within the webserver configuration needs to be verified for invalid HTTP methods.

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.28 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server mod_session_crypto Vulnerability (Dec 2016) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server mod_session_crypto Vulnerability (Dec 2016) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a vulnerability in mod_session_crypto.|insight=mod_sessioncrypto is encrypting its data/cookie using the configured ciphers with possibly either CBC or ECB modes of operation (AES256-CBC by default), hence no selectable or builtin authenticated encryption. This makes it vulnerable to padding oracle attacks, particularly with CBC. An authentication tag (SipHash MAC) is now added to prevent such attacks.|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.1 through 2.4.23.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.25 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.25 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.58 'mod_macro' Out-of-bounds Read Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.58 'mod_macro' Out-of-bounds Read Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to an out-of-bounds read vulnerability in mod_macro.|insight=|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.57 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.58 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.58 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 mod_session_cookie Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 mod_session_cookie Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N|summary=In Apache HTTP Server mod_session checks the session expiry time before decoding the session. This causes session expiry time to be ignored for mod_session_cookie sessions since the expiry time is loaded when the session is decoded.|insight=|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.38 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.38 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 'Whitespace Defects' Multiple Vulnerabilities

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 'Whitespace Defects' Multiple Vulnerabilities
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=Multiple flaws exist because the application accepted a broad pattern of unusual whitespace patterns from the user-agent, including bare CR, FF, VTAB in parsing the request line and request header lines, as well as HTAB in parsing the request line. Any bare CR present in request lines was treated as whitespace and remained in the request field member 'the_request', while a bare CR in the request header field name would be honored as whitespace, and a bare CR in the request header field value was retained the input headers array. Implied additional whitespace was accepted in the request line and prior to the ':' delimiter of any request header lines.|affected=Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x before 2.2.32 and 2.3.x through 2.4.24 prior to 2.4.25.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to conduct request smuggling, response splitting and cache pollution attacks.|solution=Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.2.32 or 2.4.25 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.2.32 or 2.4.25 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.25 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server Multiple Vulnerabilities (Sep 2014) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server Multiple Vulnerabilities (Sep 2014) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:P|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2013-5704: HTTP trailers could be used to replace HTTP headers late during request processing, potentially undoing or otherwise confusing modules that examined or modified request headers earlier. This fix adds the 'MergeTrailers' directive to restore legacy behavior.
- CVE-2014-0118: A resource consumption flaw was found in mod_deflate. If request body decompression was configured (using the 'DEFLATE' input filter), a remote attacker could cause the server to consume significant memory and/or CPU resources. The use of request body decompression is not a common configuration.
- CVE-2014-0226: A race condition was found in mod_status. An attacker able to access a public server status page on a server using a threaded MPM could send a carefully crafted request which could lead to a heap buffer overflow. Note that it is not a default or recommended configuration to have a public accessible server status page.
- CVE-2014-0231: A flaw was found in mod_cgid. If a server using mod_cgid hosted CGI scripts which did not consume standard input, a remote attacker could cause child processes to hang indefinitely, leading to denial of service.|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.2.0 through 2.2.27 and 2.4.1 through 2.4.10.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.2.29, 2.4.12 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.12 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 - 2.4.40 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 - 2.4.40 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=Apache HTTP server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities:
- A limited cross-site scripting issue affecting the mod_proxy error page. An attacker could cause the link on the error page to be malformed and instead point to a page of their choice. This would be exploitable where a server was set up with proxying enabled but was misconfigured in such a way that the Proxy Error page was displayed. (CVE-2019-10092)
- Redirects configured with mod_rewrite that were intended to be self referential might be fooled by encoded newlines and redirect instead to an unexpected URL within the request URL. (CVE-2019-10098)|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.0 to 2.4.40.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.41 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.41 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server CRLF Injection Vulnerability (Dec 2016) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server CRLF Injection Vulnerability (Dec 2016) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a CRLF injection vulnerability.|insight=Possible CRLF injection allowing HTTP response splitting attacks for sites which use mod_userdir. This issue was mitigated to prohibit CR or LF injection into the 'Location' or other outbound header key or value.|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.2.0 through 2.2.31 and 2.4.1 through 2.4.23.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.2.32, 2.4.25 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.25 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: phpLDAPadmin < 1.2.4 XSS Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: phpLDAPadmin < 1.2.4 XSS Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=phpLDAPadmin is prone to a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability.|insight=A XSS vulnerability exists in htdocs/entry_chooser.php via the form, element, rdn, or container parameter.|affected=phpLDAPadmin version 1.2.3 and prior.|impact=The flaw allows remote users to inject arbitrary web script or HTML.|solution=Update to version 1.2.4 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 1.2.3 Fixed version: 1.2.4 Installation path / port: /htdocs

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 < 2.4.42 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 < 2.4.42 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities:
- mod_rewrite CWE-601 open redirect (CVE-2020-1927)
- mod_proxy_ftp use of uninitialized value (CVE-2020-1934)|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.0 to 2.4.41.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.42 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.42 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.7 - 2.4.65 Authentication Bypass Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.7 - 2.4.65 Authentication Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to an authentication bypass vulnerability.|insight=mod_userdir+suexec bypass via AllowOverride FileInfo vulnerability in Apache HTTP Server. Users with access to use the RequestHeader directive in htaccess can cause some CGI scripts to run under an unexpected userid.|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.7 through 2.4.65.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.66 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.66 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 URL Normalization Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 URL Normalization Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N|summary=When the path component of a request URL contains multiple consecutive slashes ('/'), directives such as LocationMatch and RewriteRule must account for duplicates in regular expressions while other aspects of the servers processing will implicitly collapse them.|insight=|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.39 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.39 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.1 < 2.4.24 IP Spoofing Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.1 < 2.4.24 IP Spoofing Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to an IP address spoofing vulnerability when proxying using mod_remoteip and mod_rewrite.|insight=|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.1 to 2.4.23.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.24 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.24 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.6 - 2.4.46 Tunneling Misconfiguration Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.6 - 2.4.46 Tunneling Misconfiguration Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a tunneling misconfiguration vulnerability.|insight=mod_proxy_wstunnel configured on an URL that is not necessarily upgraded by the origin server was tunneling the whole connection regardless, thus allowing for subsequent requests on the same connection to pass through with no HTTP validation, authentication or authorization possibly configured.|affected=Apache HTTP Server versions 2.4.6 to 2.4.46 on Linux.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.48 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.48 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server Multiple Vulnerabilities (Aug 2015) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server Multiple Vulnerabilities (Aug 2015) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=Multiple flaws are due to:
- an error in 'ap_some_auth_required' function in 'server/request.c' script which does not consider that a Require directive may be associated with an authorization setting rather than an authentication setting.
- an error in chunked transfer coding implementation.|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.x before 2.4.14.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to bypass intended access restrictions in opportunistic circumstances and to cause cache poisoning or credential hijacking if an intermediary proxy is in use.|solution=Update to version 2.4.14 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.14 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_proxy_fcgi' Denial of Service Vulnerability (May 2015)

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_proxy_fcgi' Denial of Service Vulnerability (May 2015)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service vulnerability.|insight=Flaw is due to an out-of-bounds read condition in the 'handle_headers' function in mod_proxy_fcgi that is triggered as user-supplied input is not properly validated when handling responses from FastCGI servers.|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.10.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow a remote attacker to cause a denial of service via specially crafted response.|solution=Update to version 2.4.12 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.12 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_lua' Denial of Service Vulnerability -01 (May 2015)

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_lua' Denial of Service Vulnerability -01 (May 2015)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service vulnerability.|insight=Flaw is due to vulnerability in lua_websocket_read function in lua_request.c in the mod_lua module.|affected=Apache HTTP Server versions through 2.4.12.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow a remote attacker to cause a denial of service via some crafted dimension.|solution=Update to version 2.4.13 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.13 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server DoS Vulnerability (Sep 2014) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server DoS Vulnerability (Sep 2014) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=A NULL pointer deference was found in mod_cache. A malicious HTTP server could cause a crash in a caching forward proxy configuration. This crash would only be a denial of service if using a threaded MPM.|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.1 through 2.4.10.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.12 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.64 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.64 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2024-42516: HTTP response splitting
- CVE-2024-43204: SSRF with mod_headers setting Content-Type header
- CVE-2024-47252: mod_ssl error log variable escaping
- CVE-2025-49812: mod_ssl TLS upgrade attack|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.63 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.64 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=phpLDAPadmin is prone to multiple cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2019-14377: XSS in htdocs/create.php
- CVE-2019-14376: XSS in htdocs/password_checker.php
- CVE-2019-14375: XSS in htdocs/rename_form.php|affected=phpLDAPadmin version 1.2.4 and prior.|impact=The flaw allows remote users to inject arbitrary web script or HTML.|solution=Update to version 1.2.5 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.66 SSI Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.66 SSI Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a Server Side Includes (SSI) vulnerability.|insight=Apache HTTP Server with SSI enabled and mod_cgid (but not mod_cgi) passes the shell-escaped query string to #exec cmd='...' directives.|affected=Apache HTTP Server prior to version 2.4.66.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.66 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_lua' Denial of Service Vulnerability (May 2015)

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_lua' Denial of Service Vulnerability (May 2015)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service vulnerability.|insight=Flaw is due to a vulnerability in LuaAuthzProvider that is triggered if a user-supplied LUA script is supplied more than once with different arguments.|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.3.x through 2.4.10.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow a remote attacker to bypass intended access restrictions in opportunistic circumstances by leveraging multiple Require directives.|solution=Update to version 2.4.12 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.x < 2.4.66 Improper Neutralization Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.x < 2.4.66 Improper Neutralization Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to an improper neutralization vulnerability.|insight=Improper neutralization of escape, meta, or control sequences vulnerability in Apache HTTP Server through environment variables set via the Apache configuration unexpectedly superseding variables calculated by the server for CGI programs.|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.x prior to 2.4.66.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.66 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 2.4.10 Fixed version: 2.4.66 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure

nvt.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.|insight=The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as defined by RFC1323/RFC7323.|affected=TCP implementations that implement RFC1323/RFC7323.|impact=A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.|solution=To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.|vuldetect=Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.

result.description:
It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 3669712214 Packet 2: 3669713342

result.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.|insight=The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.|affected=|impact=This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.|solution=Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)|vuldetect=Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)

result.description: The following response / ICMP packet has been received: - ICMP Type: 14 - ICMP Code: 0

result.name: phpLDAPadmin Detection (HTTP)

nvt.name: phpLDAPadmin Detection (HTTP)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=HTTP based detection of phpLDAPadmin.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

Detected phpLDAPadmin
Version: 1.2.3 Location: /htdocs CPE: cpe:/a:phpldapadmin_project:phpldapadmin:1.2.3

nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Hostname determination for IP 172.30.40.5:
Hostname|Source phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet|Reverse-DNS

result.name: CPE Inventory

nvt.name: CPE Inventory
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.
Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:
After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: 172.30.40.5|cpe:/a:apache:http_server:2.4.10 172.30.40.5|cpe:/a:phpldapadmin_project:phpldapadmin:1.2.3 172.30.40.5|cpe:/o:debian:debian_linux:8

result.name: HTTP Server Banner Enumeration

nvt.name: HTTP Server Banner Enumeration
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This script tries to detect / enumerate different HTTP server banner (e.g. from a frontend, backend or proxy server) by sending various different HTTP requests (valid and invalid ones).|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
It was possible to enumerate the following HTTP server banner(s):
Server banner | Enumeration technique ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Server: Apache/2.4.10 (Debian) | Invalid HTTP 00.5 GET request (non-existent HTTP version) to '/'

result.name: HTTP Server type and version

nvt.name: HTTP Server type and version
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This script detects and reports the HTTP Server's banner which might provide the type and version of it.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
The remote HTTP Server banner is:
Server: Apache/2.4.10 (Debian)

result.name: Traceroute

nvt.name: Traceroute
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.|insight=For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.|solution_type=

result.description:
Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.30.40.5):
172.30.0.2 172.30.40.5
Network distance between scanner and target: 2

result.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting

nvt.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Best matching OS:
OS: Debian GNU/Linux 8 Version: 8 CPE: cpe:/o:debian:debian_linux:8 Found by VT: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.111067 (Operating System (OS) Detection (HTTP)) Concluded from HTTP Server banner on port 80/tcp: Server: Apache/2.4.10 (Debian) Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information

result.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check

nvt.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:A/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.|solution_type=

result.description: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an answer which means IP forwarding is enabled.

result.name: LDAP Service Detection (TCP)

nvt.name: LDAP Service Detection (TCP)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=TCP based detection of services supporting the Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP).|insight=The Lightweight Directory Access Protocol, or LDAP is an application protocol for querying and modifying directory services running over TCP/IP.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: The LDAP Server supports LDAPv3.

result.name: Services

nvt.name: Services
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This plugin performs service detection.|insight=This plugin attempts to guess which service is running on the remote port(s). For instance, it searches for a web server which could listen on another port than 80 or 443 and makes this information available for other check routines.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: A web server is running on this port

result.name: HTTP Security Headers Detection

nvt.name: HTTP Security Headers Detection
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=All known security headers are being checked on the remote web server.
On completion a report will hand back whether a specific security header has been implemented (including its value and if it is deprecated) or is missing on the target.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: Missing Headers | More Information -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Content-Security-Policy | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#content-security-policy Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy | https://scotthelme.co.uk/coop-and-coep/, Note: This is an upcoming header Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy | https://scotthelme.co.uk/coop-and-coep/, Note: This is an upcoming header Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy | https://scotthelme.co.uk/coop-and-coep/, Note: This is an upcoming header Document-Policy | https://w3c.github.io/webappsec-feature-policy/document-policy#document-policy-http-header Feature-Policy | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#feature-policy, Note: The Feature Policy header has been renamed to Permissions Policy Permissions-Policy | https://w3c.github.io/webappsec-feature-policy/#permissions-policy-http-header-field Referrer-Policy | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#referrer-policy Sec-Fetch-Dest | https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers#fetch_metadata_request_headers, Note: This is a new header supported only in newer browsers like e.g. Firefox 90 Sec-Fetch-Mode | https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers#fetch_metadata_request_headers, Note: This is a new header supported only in newer browsers like e.g. Firefox 90 Sec-Fetch-Site | https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers#fetch_metadata_request_headers, Note: This is a new header supported only in newer browsers like e.g. Firefox 90 Sec-Fetch-User | https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers#fetch_metadata_request_headers, Note: This is a new header supported only in newer browsers like e.g. Firefox 90 X-Content-Type-Options | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#x-content-type-options X-Frame-Options | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#x-frame-options X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#x-permitted-cross-domain-policies X-XSS-Protection | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#x-xss-protection, Note: Most major browsers have dropped / deprecated support for this header in 2020.

result.name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting

nvt.name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=The script consolidates and reports various information for web application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.
This information is based on the following scripts / settings:
- HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)
- No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)
- Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)
- Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)
- The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use
- The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and 'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the 'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use
If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community forum.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
The Hostname/IP "phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet" was used to access the remote host.
Generic web application scanning is disabled for this host via the "Enable generic web application scanning" option within the "Global variable settings" of the scan config in use.
Requests to this service are done via HTTP/1.1.
This service seems to be able to host PHP scripts.
This service seems to be able to host ASP scripts.
The User-Agent "Mozilla/5.0 [en] (X11, U; OpenVAS-VT <version replaced>)" was used to access the remote host.
Historic /scripts and /cgi-bin are not added to the directories used for web application scanning. You can enable this again with the "Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning" option within the "Global variable settings" of the scan config in use.
A possible recursion was detected during web application scanning:
The service is using a relative URL in one or more HTML references where e.g. /file1.html contains <a href="subdir/file2.html"> and a subsequent request for subdir/file2.html is linking to subdir/file2.html. This would resolves to subdir/subdir/file2.html causing a recursion. To work around this counter-measures have been enabled but the service should be fixed as well to not use such problematic links. Below an excerpt of URLs is shown to help identify those issues.
Syntax : URL (HTML link)
http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs/ (css/default/style.css) http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs/ (images/default/bug-big.png) http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs/ (images/default/forum-big.png) http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs/ (images/default/help-big.png) http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs/ (images/default/login.png)
The following directories were used for web application scanning:
http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/ http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/config http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/doc http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/lib http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/templates http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/tools
While this is not, in and of itself, a bug, you should manually inspect these directories to ensure that they are in compliance with company security standards
The following directories were excluded from web application scanning because the "Regex pattern to exclude directories from CGI scanning" setting of the VT "Global variable settings" (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.12288) for this scan was: "/(index\.php|image|img|css|js$|js/|javascript|style|theme|icon|jquery|graphic|grafik|picture|bilder|thumbnail|media/|skins?/)"
http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs/css/default http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs/images http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs/images/default http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs/js http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs/js/jscalendar http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/icons
The following CGIs were discovered:
Syntax : cginame (arguments [default value])
http://phpldapadmin-remote-dump.trabalho_vulnnet/htdocs/cmd.php (server_id [1] cmd [purge_cache] )

nvt.name: FCKeditor Detection (HTTP)
